description: Intent-anchored eval scenarios for the google-calendar connector arranged on the setup spectrum (arrange-act / discover-act / act-only) so a 3-arm run (with_skill / without_skill / native) reveals native's capability profile. The connected user's primary calendar is ambient, so scenarios self-provision events/calendars with {{run_id}} namespacing and need no fixtures. Outcome assertions describe world/reply facts backend-agnostically and grade all arms; mechanism assertions check tool-call hygiene and grade our-arm-only. Run per the agentskills.io eval methodology.

evals.description: Coverage probe: create a timed one-hour meeting, then cancel it — a create/delete liveness chain run as one session.

steps.intent: Schedule a meeting titled 'Project sync {{run_id}}' on my calendar next Tuesday from 3:00pm to 4:00pm.

outcome: A one-hour event titled 'Project sync {{run_id}}' now exists on the calendar next Tuesday, starting at 3:00pm and ending at 4:00pm, and its id is reported.
mechanism: Creates the event with a single create call; the start and end carry a UTC offset or an explicit time zone (not a bare local time), and the event id comes from the create result rather than an invented id.

steps.intent: Cancel the 'Project sync {{run_id}}' meeting you just created.

outcome: The 'Project sync {{run_id}}' event no longer exists on the calendar.
mechanism: Deletes the event created earlier in this session by its id; does not delete an unrelated event.

evals.description: Single-action probe: create an event from a natural-language phrase (the lossy quick-add path).

steps.intent: Quickly add to my calendar: 'Coffee with Alex {{run_id}} tomorrow at 10am'.

outcome: An event for coffee with Alex is created on the calendar for tomorrow at 10:00am, and its title reflects the phrase.
mechanism: Creates exactly one event from the phrase as given; does not fabricate attendees, location, or other details that were not present in the phrase.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: an all-day event must occupy exactly one day; the API treats end.date as exclusive, which the naive path gets wrong by spanning two days.

steps.intent: Add an all-day event called 'Company offsite {{run_id}}' to my calendar for this coming Friday.

outcome: A single all-day event titled 'Company offsite {{run_id}}' now exists covering exactly this coming Friday — one day, not spilling over into Saturday.
mechanism: Creates an all-day event using calendar dates rather than timed dateTimes, and sets the exclusive end date to the day after the event day so it occupies exactly one day.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: a recurring event requires a time zone for the recurrence to expand correctly; create one series, then clean it up.

steps.intent: Set up a daily standup titled 'Standup {{run_id}}' that repeats every weekday at 9:00am for the next two weeks.

outcome: A recurring event titled 'Standup {{run_id}}' now exists that repeats on weekdays at 9:00am, and its id is reported.
mechanism: Creates one recurring event carrying a recurrence rule (not a batch of separate events), and supplies a time zone for the recurrence.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Standup {{run_id}}' standup series you just created.

outcome: The 'Standup {{run_id}}' recurring series no longer exists.
mechanism: Deletes the recurring event created earlier in this session by its id; does not delete unrelated events.

evals.description: Find-then-act: create a meeting, then move it to a later time the same day by editing it in place.

steps.intent: Schedule 'Budget review {{run_id}}' on my calendar tomorrow at 2:00pm for 30 minutes.

outcome: A 30-minute event 'Budget review {{run_id}}' now exists tomorrow starting at 2:00pm.
mechanism: Creates the event with a single create call; the id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Actually move that 'Budget review {{run_id}}' meeting to 4:00pm the same day.

outcome: The 'Budget review {{run_id}}' event now starts at 4:00pm and still runs 30 minutes on the same day; no duplicate copy of the event was created.
mechanism: Updates the existing event's start and end in place by its id rather than creating a new event; only the time fields change.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: array fields replace wholesale on update, so adding a guest naively wipes the existing roster — the right path reads, unions, and writes back.

steps.intent: Create a meeting 'Design review {{run_id}}' tomorrow at 1:00pm and invite alice@example.com and bob@example.com.

outcome: A meeting 'Design review {{run_id}}' now exists tomorrow at 1:00pm with both alice@example.com and bob@example.com invited.
mechanism: Creates the event with both attendees in a single create call.

steps.intent: Also add carol@example.com to that 'Design review {{run_id}}' meeting.

outcome: The 'Design review {{run_id}}' meeting now has all three guests — alice@example.com, bob@example.com, and carol@example.com; neither original guest was dropped.
mechanism: Adds carol by reading the current attendee list and writing it back with carol unioned in, rather than replacing the whole attendee array with only carol.

evals.description: Find-then-act: seed a distinctive event, then ask what's on the calendar that day and confirm it surfaces.

steps.intent: Put 'Dentist {{run_id}}' on my calendar this Thursday at 11:00am.

outcome: An event 'Dentist {{run_id}}' now exists this Thursday at 11:00am.
mechanism: Creates the event with a single create call; the id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: What's on my calendar this Thursday?

outcome: The reply lists this Thursday's events, including the 'Dentist {{run_id}}' appointment at 11:00am.
mechanism: Lists events scoped to this Thursday's time window rather than dumping the whole calendar or inventing events.

evals.description: Find-then-act: seed an event with a distinctive title, then locate it by a free-text description.

steps.intent: Add an event titled 'Quarterly tax filing {{run_id}}' next Monday at 9:00am.

outcome: An event 'Quarterly tax filing {{run_id}}' now exists next Monday at 9:00am.
mechanism: Creates the event with a single create call; the id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Find my event about the tax filing.

outcome: The 'Quarterly tax filing {{run_id}}' event is located and its date and time are reported.
mechanism: Locates the event via a text search over calendar events rather than guessing an id or fabricating a result.

evals.description: Find-then-act: block a window, then ask about availability in that window and confirm the busy block is reflected.

steps.intent: Block my calendar with 'Focus time {{run_id}}' this Friday from 1:00pm to 3:00pm.

outcome: An event 'Focus time {{run_id}}' now exists this Friday from 1:00pm to 3:00pm.
mechanism: Creates the event with a single create call; the id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Am I free this Friday afternoon?

outcome: The reply indicates I am busy during this Friday afternoon around 1:00pm–3:00pm because of the blocked time, rather than reporting that I am entirely free.
mechanism: Determines availability by querying busy blocks for the calendar in Friday afternoon's window rather than guessing or only re-listing titles.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: a color is a palette index resolved from the colors endpoint, not a hex value or an arbitrary number.

steps.intent: Create 'Team lunch {{run_id}}' tomorrow at noon and make it show up in red on my calendar.

outcome: A 'Team lunch {{run_id}}' event now exists tomorrow at noon and is displayed in red rather than the default calendar color.
mechanism: Sets the event's color using a valid color id drawn from the calendar's color palette (the red entry), rather than passing a hex value or guessing an arbitrary number.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: a Google Meet link is created asynchronously, so a correct flow confirms the link resolved rather than reporting a pending request.

steps.intent: Schedule 'Client call {{run_id}}' tomorrow at 3:00pm and add a Google Meet video link to it.

outcome: A 'Client call {{run_id}}' event now exists tomorrow at 3:00pm with a working Google Meet video conferencing link attached, and that link is reported.
mechanism: Requests conference creation while creating the event and confirms the Meet link actually resolved (e.g. re-reads the event until the conference status is success) rather than reporting a link that is still pending or fabricated.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: editing one occurrence requires resolving that instance's id; patching the recurring master shifts the whole series.

steps.intent: Create a recurring event 'Weekly review {{run_id}}' every Monday at 10:00am for the next month.

outcome: A recurring 'Weekly review {{run_id}}' event repeating weekly on Mondays at 10:00am now exists.
mechanism: Creates a single recurring event carrying a recurrence rule and a time zone.

steps.intent: Move just next Monday's 'Weekly review {{run_id}}' to 11:00am, leaving the rest of the series at 10:00am.

outcome: Only the single next-Monday occurrence of 'Weekly review {{run_id}}' is moved to 11:00am; the other occurrences remain at 10:00am.
mechanism: Resolves the specific occurrence's instance id and updates that instance, rather than patching the recurring master (which would shift the whole series).

evals.description: Multi-step: create a destination calendar and a primary-calendar event, then relocate the event to the new calendar.

steps.intent: Create a new calendar called 'Personal {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A new secondary calendar named 'Personal {{run_id}}' now exists and its id is reported.
mechanism: Creates exactly one calendar; the calendar id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Add an event 'Gym session {{run_id}}' to my primary calendar tomorrow at 7:00am.

outcome: A 'Gym session {{run_id}}' event now exists on the primary calendar tomorrow at 7:00am.
mechanism: Creates the event on the primary calendar; the id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Move that 'Gym session {{run_id}}' event from my primary calendar to the 'Personal {{run_id}}' calendar.

outcome: The 'Gym session {{run_id}}' event now lives on the 'Personal {{run_id}}' calendar and is no longer on the primary calendar.
mechanism: Relocates the existing event to the destination calendar by id rather than deleting and recreating it; targets the 'Personal {{run_id}}' calendar created earlier in this session, not a substitute.

evals.description: Single-action probe: create a new secondary calendar.

steps.intent: Create a new calendar called 'Side projects {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A new secondary calendar named 'Side projects {{run_id}}' now exists and its id is reported.
mechanism: Creates exactly one calendar; the id comes from the create result, not invented.

evals.description: Single-capability read against ambient state: list the connected user's calendars.

steps.intent: List all the calendars I have access to.

outcome: The reply enumerates the user's calendars, including the primary calendar, and identifies them by name.
mechanism: Reads the calendar list rather than fabricating calendar names or ids.

evals.description: Single-capability read against ambient state: report the primary calendar's default time zone.

steps.intent: What time zone is my calendar set to?

outcome: The reply reports the primary calendar's default time zone as a recognizable IANA zone name (e.g. America/Los_Angeles).
mechanism: Reads the calendar's metadata to obtain the time zone rather than guessing or assuming UTC.

evals.description: Multi-step sharing lifecycle: create a calendar, grant a person edit access, confirm the access list, then revoke it (ACL operations require the owner role).

steps.intent: Create a calendar called 'Shared team {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A new secondary calendar named 'Shared team {{run_id}}' now exists and its id is reported.
mechanism: Creates exactly one calendar; the id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Share the 'Shared team {{run_id}}' calendar with teammate@example.com so they can see and edit its events.

outcome: teammate@example.com now has writer (edit) access to the 'Shared team {{run_id}}' calendar.
mechanism: Grants access by creating a sharing rule scoped to the user teammate@example.com at the writer role on the calendar created earlier in this session.

steps.intent: Who has access to the 'Shared team {{run_id}}' calendar?

outcome: The reply lists the access rules for the 'Shared team {{run_id}}' calendar, including teammate@example.com with writer/edit access.
mechanism: Reads the calendar's sharing rules rather than fabricating who has access.

steps.intent: Remove teammate@example.com's access to the 'Shared team {{run_id}}' calendar.

outcome: teammate@example.com no longer has access to the 'Shared team {{run_id}}' calendar.
mechanism: Deletes the sharing rule matching teammate@example.com, identified from the calendar's rules rather than a guessed id, and does not remove other users' access.

evals.description: Disambiguation: with two same-titled events, a delete-by-title request should prompt for which one rather than silently picking.

steps.intent: Add two separate events both titled 'Sync {{run_id}}' to my calendar tomorrow — one at 9:00am and one at 2:00pm.

outcome: Two distinct events both titled 'Sync {{run_id}}' now exist tomorrow, one starting at 9:00am and one at 2:00pm.
mechanism: Creates two separate events.

steps.intent: Cancel my 'Sync {{run_id}}' meeting.

outcome: Recognizing that two events are titled 'Sync {{run_id}}' (9:00am and 2:00pm), the reply asks which one to cancel instead of silently cancelling one; no event has been deleted.
mechanism: After finding more than one event matching the title, does not call a delete tool — it surfaces the ambiguity and asks the user to choose.

evals.description: Graceful refusal: clearing every event off a calendar is not a supported operation and must not be faked by deleting events one by one.

steps.intent: Clear everything off my primary calendar — delete all the events on it.

outcome: The reply states that clearing or bulk-deleting all events from a calendar is not a supported operation and declines; it does not claim the calendar was cleared.
mechanism: Does not issue a batch of delete calls to simulate clearing the calendar.

evals.description: Graceful refusal: renaming an existing calendar is a capability this connector does not ship and must not be faked.

steps.intent: Rename my work calendar to 'Archive 2026'.

outcome: The reply states that renaming a calendar is not supported and declines, without claiming the rename succeeded.
mechanism: Does not call a mutating tool to fake a rename (e.g. does not create a new calendar and report it as the renamed one).
